text.title: typescript-strict-mode Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for typescript-strict-mode as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using any or as any / as unknown as Foo to silence a type error
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Bypasses strict mode entirely for that value; downstream code loses type checking. Use unknown + type guards instead.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hides real null bugs; if any value in the chain is null at runtime, you get an unhandled TypeError with no useful message.

failure-mode.description: Using @ts-ignore to silence errors that accumulate over time
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: @ts-ignore never expires; when the underlying issue is fixed the suppression stays. Use @ts-expect-error — it errors out when no longer needed.

failure-mode.description: Returning Promise<any> from a service function
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Consumer types collapse to any; the entire downstream chain loses strictness.

failure-mode.description: Using Partial<T> as a default for update payloads or config objects
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Reduces strictness to "everything optional"; callers can pass empty objects with no error. Model update types explicitly with only the mutable fields.
